_doc: Per-rule upstream-parity enforcement level for the synced @eslint/markdown fixtures (see test/upstream.test.mjs). Levels: 'full' (the default for any rule not listed here) = exact parity is enforced for every valid and invalid case — each reported error's messageId, data, and 1-indexed location must match upstream, and cases declaring `output` must autofix to the same text. 'off' = quarantined; the Rust core still diverges from upstream for this rule, so its valid and invalid cases are registered as skipped (coverage stays visible) until the divergence is fixed and the rule is promoted to 'full'. The end state is zero 'off' entries. Each quarantined rule carries a `note` summarising the known divergence to guide the follow-up fix PR. Regenerate fixtures with `pnpm run port:tests:eslint-markdown`.

fenced-code-language.note: Diagnostic span points at the language token; upstream reports the opening code-fence span (node.position.start, line through the info string).

fenced-code-meta.note: Diagnostic span points at the meta token; upstream reports the opening code-fence span.

heading-increment.note: Diverges on headings inside HTML blocks / blockquotes and on TOML (+++) and JSON frontmatter title detection.

no-bare-urls.note: False positives on URLs inside link/definition labels, HTML attributes, and existing autolinks.

no-duplicate-definitions.note: Treats deeply-indented (code-block) lines as definitions; upstream parses them as indented code.

no-duplicate-headings.note: Heading-text normalisation differs (closed ATX trailing '#', collapsed inner spaces).

no-empty-definitions.note: Footnote multi-line content and escaped-caret labels classified differently from upstream.

no-html.note: Flags HTML tags inside `<!-- -->` comments; upstream ignores comment contents.

no-invalid-label-refs.note: False positives on collapsed `[foo][]` / `![foo][]` references with a matching definition.

no-missing-atx-heading-space.note: PANICS on non-ASCII input (char-boundary slice at lib.rs position_for_offset); span/column divergences.

no-missing-label-refs.note: Broad divergence in shortcut/collapsed reference resolution.

no-missing-link-fragments.note: Broad divergence in heading-anchor slugging and fragment matching.

no-multiple-h1.note: Frontmatter-title (YAML/TOML/JSON) interaction with the first H1 differs from upstream.

no-reference-like-urls.note: Broad divergence detecting inline URLs that collide with definition identifiers.

no-reversed-media-syntax.note: Broad divergence detecting reversed `(text)[url]` link/image syntax.

no-space-in-emphasis.note: Fundamentally different emphasis-marker scan; large divergence in spans and counts.

no-unused-definitions.note: Reference-usage tracking differs for footnotes and collapsed references.

require-alt-text.note: Divergence detecting images nested in HTML and reference-style images without alt text.

table-column-count.note: GFM table cell counting and diagnostic span diverge from upstream.
